home.title: Gabriel Habermann
home.subTitle: AWS Certified Software Engineer with 6 years of experience in cloud solution development and architecture.

about.p1: Graduated in Computer Science from UNESP and certified as an AWS Cloud Practitioner, I started my career as a developer in 2017 while still in high school and technical education.
about.p2: Since then, I have been working as a backend software engineer, developing solutions in the .NET ecosystem (Core and Framework) with C#. I work with technologies such as Kubernetes, Docker, and MySQL, applying best practices like SOLID and KISS to ensure clean and maintainable code.
about.p3: Although my primary focus is backend development, I also have full-stack experience using React on the front end. I hold strong knowledge in unit testing with xUnit and NUnit, and hands-on expertise delivering software through the AWS environment.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtext: tools and technologies I work with

0.title: Why I chose .NET for microservices
0.preview: After evaluating Go, Node.js and .NET for a distributed system, the rich ecosystem, strong typing, and performance of .NET made it the obvious choice for our team.

1.title: My Kubernetes learning journey
1.preview: From zero to production-ready in 3 months: deployments, services, ingress controllers, Helm charts, and everything I wish I had known from the start.

2.title: SOLID in practice — real examples
2.preview: Abstract principles only make sense when you see them break. Here are the concrete refactors that made SOLID click for me after 3 years of writing C#.

3.title: Notes from the AWS Cloud Practitioner exam
3.preview: Key concepts, the services that actually matter, and the mental model I built to pass the certification with confidence.
